title: DynAgent — Phase 6 (AugErrSum 5-term + η^op + X^DR + invariant violations)
description: DynAgent v2 Phase 6 Task 6e + 6g completion dashboard. Surfaces the (AugErrSum) 6-term ratio panel (Phase 3 4-term + Phase 5 eHist + Phase 6g rVis) + η^op + X^DR (Phase 5d/5e) + Phase 4 invariant-violations breakdown + Phase 6g three-tier visualisation breakdown (L1 type / L2 invariant / L3 integration) + Phase 5 Haskell-core request counters.

panels.title: C0354 — (AugErrSum) 4-term ratio panel (Phase 3 / source: App FastAPI)
E_rep — representation error ratio
panels.description: Phase 2 (AugErrSum). Mode A/B/C/E + llm_parse_error events / RecursiveDecomposition initiations. Source: App GET /api/sessions/metrics/aug-err-sum -> eRep.ratio.

panels.title: E_ver — verification error ratio
panels.description: Phase 2 (AugErrSum). materialize_handoff_failed + artifact_owner_violation + ... / denominator. Source: App GET /api/sessions/metrics/aug-err-sum -> eVer.ratio.

panels.title: E_dec — decomposition error ratio
panels.description: Phase 3 (AugErrSum). depth_exceeded + replay_detected + may_decompose_false + sub_workflow_compile_failed + materialization_incomplete + agent_fallback + approval_gate_unavailable + auto_denied + degraded_retry_failed / denominator. Source: App GET /api/sessions/metrics/aug-err-sum -> eDec.ratio.

panels.title: R_int — intervention reduction ratio
panels.description: Phase 1 (AugErrSum). degraded_retry_succeeded + auto_approved / denominator. Higher is better. Source: App GET /api/sessions/metrics/aug-err-sum -> rInt.ratio.

panels.title: Phase 5 additions: E_hist + η^op + X^DR (source: App FastAPI + Prometheus)
E_hist — history error ratio
panels.description: Phase 5 (AugErrSum). SavedPlanningState 欠損 + HistorySnapshot missing-trial counts + cross-step state divergence / denominator. Source: App GET /api/sessions/metrics/aug-err-sum -> eHist.ratio.

panels.title: η^op — operational efficiency (Python aggregate)
panels.description: Phase 5 Task 5d. R_int.numerator / Σ(error term numerators). Higher is better. Source: App GET /api/sessions/metrics/eta-op -> value. Parallel implementation to Haskell POST /experiment/eta-op (dashboard reads Python path to avoid Haskell process dependency for the visualisation surface).

panels.title: η^op — Prometheus gauge (last-call value on Haskell /experiment/eta-op)
panels.description: Phase 5 Task 5d. Haskell core writes the most recent computed η^op into the dynagent_experiment_eta_op_value gauge on every POST. NaN encodes 'undefined'. Use this panel side-by-side with the Python-aggregated panel #21 to verify Haskell ↔ Python parity (D-18 candidate verification).

panels.title: X^DR — last DR contrast (Python AIPW)
panels.description: Phase 5 Task 5e. POST /api/sessions/metrics/xcross-doubly-robust returns per-arm DR contrasts (AIPW when features non-empty, Hájek IPW fallback otherwise). This panel displays the last-computed treatment-vs-baseline contrast point_estimate. Panel value is a stub until the Phase 6 Task 6e orchestrator wires periodic POSTs from a scheduled job (Task 6.post candidate).

targets._note: Body must carry a non-empty samples array; Phase 6 Task 6.post wires a scheduled producer.

panels.title: R_vis — visualisation reduction (Phase 6 Task 6g)
panels.description: Phase 6 Task 6g (Ch 18.4 three-tier failure visualisation). R_vis = (L2 Warning + L3 Info violations) / total visualisation-observable steps. L1 Critical violations are reported in panel #25 for transparency but NOT in the numerator (those belong in E_* terms — see Categorical/Visualisation.hs module header for the layer ↔ severity binding). Higher is better. Source: App GET /api/sessions/metrics/visualization -> value.

panels.title: R_vis three-tier breakdown (L1 / L2 / L3 counts)
panels.description: Phase 6 Task 6g. Per-tier violation counts that feed R_vis: L1 (Critical / type-level), L2 (Warning / invariant-property), L3 (Info / integration). L1 is shown for operator awareness — Critical violations belong in E_* terms (E_rep / E_ver / E_dec / E_hist) and are excluded from the R_vis numerator. Surfaces Ch 18.4 three-tier structure end-to-end on the dashboard. Source: App GET /api/sessions/metrics/visualization -> {l1_count, l2_count, l3_count}.

panels.title: Phase 4 invariant violations + Phase 5 Haskell-core request rates
Invariant violation breakdown by triage outcome
panels.description: Phase 4 Task 4d. (abort / log_continue / log_only / ok) buckets over the dashboard time window. Phase 4 is advisory-only (abort_count is informational, no Stop(InvariantViolated) raise). Phase 6 Task 6.post + C0358 candidate wires the production flow swap once the abort base-rate is measured.

panels.title: Haskell core request rate (5-min average)
panels.description: Phase 0 Task 0j + Phase 5 Task 5c. Prometheus counter rate over the dashboard window. Surfaces (a) operator-visible throughput by endpoint, (b) Phase 6 Task 6f alert correlation (step request p95 > 5s rule reads the same counter family). Custom-metric HPA scaling (Task 6d hpa.yaml) reads dynagent_step_requests_total — this panel surfaces the same series.

panels.title: Cumulative invariant violations emitted (counter)
panels.description: Phase 4 Task 4d. dynagent_invariant_violations_total — monotonic counter incremented once per Violation emitted by /check-invariants. Spike pattern indicates a (Trans-N) regression hot zone; correlate with panel #30 to identify which triage outcome dominates the surge.
